title: Polyscias lydgatei
intro: Polyscias lydgatei, also known as the Hawaiian holly fern, is a medicinal plant native to the Hawaiian Islands, belonging to the family Araliaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Polyscias lydgatei include flavonoids, sesquiterpenoids, and lignans, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Polyscias lydgatei is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture to treat fever, sore throats, and skin irritations, as well as being used topically to soothe wounds and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Polyscias lydgatei is a species of flowering plant in the family Araliaceae. This plant is part of the genus Polyscias, which includes several other species of trees and shrubs. The classification of Polyscias lydgatei is further divided into two subcategories: the kingdom Plantae and the order Apiales. The family Araliaceae is a group of plants that are commonly known as the ginseng or ivy family. Within this family, Polyscias lydgatei is a unique species with distinct characteristics.
uses: Polyscias lydgatei is a plant species native to Hawaii, and its medicinal uses are primarily based on traditional knowledge. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In traditional Hawaiian medicine, the roots and leaves of Polyscias lydgatei are used to treat various ailments, including fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. However, further scientific research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of these uses. More studies are required to fully understand the potential health benefits and applications of Polyscias lydgatei.

The plant also possesses cardiotonic and anti-diarrheal activities, suggesting its potential use in treating cardiovascular and gastrointestinal issues.
